observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our audit recommendations that the LCE -
a) instruct the Property Officer and Accounting Office to prepare a reconciliation of their records and to perform the cleansing count eventually; (3)
b) order the Inventory Committee to tag all PPEs with inventory stickers for easy identification; (4)
c) direct the Accounting Office to exert more effort to reconcile PPELCs with the GLs; (5)
d) advise the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory taking for the ensuing year and prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. (6)

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the LCE -
a) instruct the concerned Offices to be mindful of their duties laid down in the said Circular; (7)
b) require the Property Officer to come up with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction; (8)
c) require the Accounting Office to exert effort in preparing the necessary records and to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and (9)
d) instruct the Municipal Engineer to assist and/or coordinate in the establishment of correct and verifiable Roadwork Network System. (10)

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the LCE direct the Accounting Office to exert effort in –
a) tracing the cause of the disparity of the RPT and SET Receivable balances and record any necessary adjustments affecting the counterpart deferred income; (13)
b) locating documents relative to the dormant accounts to verify the validity of the collectibles. (14)
We further recommended that the posted transactions to the Advances to Officers and Employees be analyzed by the Accounting Office to determine the particulars of the settled amount of ₱57,373.88. (15)
We also reiterated the recommendation that the LCE enjoin the Accounting Office to –
a) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts recorded as payables; and (16)
b) henceforth, prepare and maintain SLs per creditor necessary to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements. (17)
Lastly, we recommended that LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to implement COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts. (18)

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the:
a) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to lead the -
 i)monitoring of the implementation of the CDP/LDIP; (19)
 and (20)
 ii) undertaking of prioritization of PPAs so that specific dates of implementation are indicated in the LDIP/AIP; and (20)
b) Accounting Office to be careful in using accounts in the recognition of transactions; (21)
We also recommended that POWs for the PPAs implemented by the barangays be prepared by the ME and approved by the Punong Barangay and the LCE and consequently, inspection reports should be prepared by the ME and approved also by the two heads of the LGUs concerned before payment to ensure that the POWs are properly prepared, the parties had meeting of minds on the PPAs to be implemented, and that the projects are correctly completed. (22)
We further recommended that the LCE instruct the
 a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and other concerned officials and employees to submit necessary documents and/or explanations for the audit observations presented in paragraph 7.43; (23)
 b) Concerned personnel to submit an explanation on the determined ineligible PPAs funded by the DF; (24)
 c) Barangays to submit the required documents enumerated in Article 2.B.4 and 2.B.6 of the MOA; (25)
 d) LPMC and other concerned parties to review and evaluate the stipulations contained in the MOA and effect necessary actions when so warranted and e Accounting Office to review the transactions pertaining to the transfer of funds to barangays and effect necessary journal entries. (26)

We reiterate our recommendation that the LCE:
a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to -
 i) be cautious in the preparation of accounting entries and reporting of LDRRMF so as not to mislead stakeholders and cause inappropriate decisions; (27)
 ii)follow the MNGAS for LGUs in the recognition of procurement of supplies and materials; and (28)
 iii)record necessary adjustment for the CY 2020 cash donation received as recommended in the CY 2020 Audit Report; (29)
b) instruct the LDRRMO to -
 i) act on the compliance with the minimum standards of disaster preparedness; (30)
 ii) and design the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format by indicating the targeted date of implementation of the PPAs; (31)
c) caution the Municipal Accountant, MBO, and MT not to be remiss in their responsibilities in the monitoring of the LDRRMFs and to reconcile their records immediately and effect necessary adjustments about the erroneous charging of the project Improvement of MDRRM Operation Center; (32)
d) direct the BAC, BAC secretariat, the Municipal Accountant and the end users to submit explanations on the noted post audit observations in CY 2022 AAR. (33)
We further recommended that:
a) for future similar transactions, the LCE instruct the concerned officers to attach to the DVs, distribution lists completely indicating necessary details to facilitate the conduct of validation; (34)
b) the LDRRMO review implemented projects and ensure that only allowable expenses resulting from disaster risk management activities are charged from LDRRMF; and (35)
c) concerned officers submit explanation or justification on noted deficiencies in paragraph 8.39. (36)

We recommend that the Accounting Office dedicate its utmost efforts towards completing the essential records. This will not only streamline the monitoring of received funds but also enhance the effectiveness of decision-making processes. (37)
We further recommend that the LCE evaluate the personnel complement of the Accounting Office and implement remedial actions if necessary. (38)

We recommended that the project focal person/s –
a) coordinate with the finance committee for each project proposal submitted to funding agencies, ensuring that necessary counterpart funds are identified and secured so that the projects can be immediately initiated as planned; (39)
b) engage with the BAC during the procurement process to minimize, if not eliminate, factors contributing to delays. (40)
We further recommended that the LCE review the manpower complement of offices vis-à-vis the programs each office has to handle, and implement any necessary solution or remedy for the proper and timely execution of projects. (41)
Likewise, we recommended that the project proposal for the Establishment of a Pigmented Rice Production Facility through the Adoption of a Brown Rice Impeller Machine be reviewed and coordinated with the DOST for any necessary adjustments to attain the project’s objectives. (42)

We recommended that the LCE instruct the accountable officers of the Municipality to
a) comply with the provisions of the MNGAS and LTOM on the preparation of RAAF and CRAAF for the proper monitoring and evaluation of their accountabilities over accountable forms; (43)
b) prepare RIS for every requisition of accountable form booklet to completely document the issuance. (44)

We reiterate our recommendation that the LSB and the Property Officer coordinate with the DepEd regarding securing a separate clearance from property accountability from the Municipality by the DepEd employees as a pre-requisite to the issuance of DepEd office clearance. (45)
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the a) Inventory Committee to count the PPEs of the SEF and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE; (46)
b) Property Officer to – i) issue applicable ICS and /or ARE/PAR for all procured inventories and property; (47) and ii) prepare and maintain PCs for the SEF PPEs; (48)
c) Accounting Office to prepare and maintain complete SEF PPELCs; and (49)
d) BAC, BAC Secretariat, Accounting Office, Engineering Office and other concerned offices to submit explanation on the audit observations presented in paragraph 12.11. (50)

We recommended that the MTO examine the books of more businesses applying for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the provisions of the LGC and LTOM to properly assess the tax liabilities of the said establishments. (53)

We recommended that the LCE and other concerned offices evaluate the necessity and importance of MENRO and consider creating a plantilla item in the following budget preparation. (56)
Likewise, we reiterated the audit recommendation that the SWMP be synchronized with annual budgets for proper monitoring and evaluation. (57)

We recommended that the LCE and other concerned Offices
a) assess the manpower allocation within the MAO in relation to the on-going projects being implemented by the said Office; (58)
b) identify significant issues and concerns potentially affecting the office’s performance and projects’ operations; and (59)
c) apply effective solutions to address identified problems (60)

We reiterated our recommendation that the MT and Municipal Agriculturist submit complete documents and accounting of the transactions related to the three bank accounts from the time they became depositor up to the present, for evaluation purposes. (63)

We recommended that the GFPS ensure that attribution is made in accordance with the HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Municipality’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.(66)
We also recommended that the GFPS constitute its M & E Team to execute a proper monitoring and evaluation system. (67)
We further recommended that –
a) detailed guidelines for the dispersal of farm inputs be developed and officially endorsed; and (68)

observations.managementAction: RA 7160 directs LGUs to organized City Development Council in line with the national policy of encouraging an implementation of comprehensive multi-sectoral development plans. However, only barangays that complied proper liquidation are granted with fund transfers.

There were partial release of funds to BSP and GSP in 2023 that have been subsequently liquidated related to scouting activities of the various public schools in Iligan City.

Moving forward, we will ensure that support to scouting activities of the students will be coursed through directly to DepEd or through the concerned schools.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Accounting Office to prepare BRS for all the bank accounts maintained by the Municipality in order to capture all transpired transactions that need to be recognized in the books of accounts.
The Accounting Office agreed to prepare BRS for all the bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to be cautious in the preparation of BRS and in the identification of items or amounts that should be adjusted in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
The Accounting Office agreed to be cautious in the preparation of BRS in the future.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to
adjust the books of accounts of the Municipality for the double take-up of the fund received from the DOE; and
henceforth, be cautious in the recognition of transactions.
The Accounting Office informed the Audit Team that the double recording of the DOE fund was adjusted in March 2024 upon receipt of the audit memorandum.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the LCE direct the Accounting Office to exert effort in -
tracing the cause of the disparity of the RPT and SET Receivable balances and record any necessary adjustments affecting the counterpart deferred income; and
locating documents relative to the dormant accounts to verify the validity of the collectibles.
We further recommended that the posted transactions to the Advances to Officers and Employees be analyzed by the Accounting Office to determine the particulars of the settled amount of ₱57,373.88.
We also reiterated the recommendation that the LCE enjoin the Accounting Office to -
exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts recorded as payables; and
henceforth, prepare and maintain SLs per creditor necessary to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements.
Lastly, we recommended that LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to implement COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts.
The Accounting Office committed to implement the audit recommendations regarding the Receivable and Payable accounts, except for the maintenance of complete SLs.

We recommended that the Accounting Office dedicate its utmost efforts towards completing the essential records. This will not only streamline the monitoring of received funds but also enhance the effectiveness of decision-making processes.
The Municipal Accountant commented that there is a need for an additional staff to tackle the task of maintenance of SLs, thus the complete preparation and maintenance of the said record in CY 2024 was not assured.
We further recommended that the LCE evaluate the personnel complement of the Accounting Office and implement remedial actions if necessary.
The Management stated that the Municipality has limited budget for PS, hence, additional staff for the Accounting Office is not guaranteed in CY 2024.
Likewise, we recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC, BAC Secretariat, and other concerned officials and employees to submit necessary documents and/or explanations for the audit observations presented in paragraph 9.6.
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation in the preceding paragraph.

We recommended that the project focal person/s –
coordinate with the finance committee for each project proposal submitted to funding agencies, ensuring that necessary counterpart funds are identified and secured so that the projects can be immediately initiated as planned; and
engage with the BAC during the procurement process to minimize, if not entirely eliminate, factors contributing to delays.
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations mentioned in paragraph 10.11.
We further recommended that the LCE review the manpower complement of offices vis-à-vis the programs each office has to handle, and implement any necessary solution or remedy for the proper and timely execution of projects.
The Management explained that the Municipality cannot just hire additional personnel because of PS budget limitation.
Likewise, we recommended that the project proposal for the Establishment of a Pigmented Rice Production Facility through the Adoption of a Brown Rice Impeller Machine be reviewed and coordinated with the DOST for any necessary adjustments to attain the project’s objectives.
The MAO commented that he had partially coordinated with the DOST necessary adjustments for completing reports and attaining the project objectives.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the accountable officers of the Municipality to -
comply with the provisions of the MNGAS and LTOM on the preparation of RAAF and CRAAF for the proper monitoring and evaluation of their accountabilities over accountable forms; and
prepare RIS for every requisition of accountable form booklet to completely document the issuance.
The Accountable Officers of the Municipality agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LSB and the Property Officer coordinate with the DepEd regarding securing a separate clearance from property accountability from the Municipality by the DepEd employees as a pre-requisite to the issuance of DepEd office clearance.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the –
Inventory Committee to count the PPEs of the SEF and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE; and
Property Officer to –
issue applicable ICS and /or ARE/PAR for all procured inventories and property; and
prepare and maintain PCs for the SEF PPEs;
Accounting Office to prepare and maintain complete SEF PPELCs; and
BAC, BAC Secretariat, Accounting Office, Engineering Office and other concerned offices to submit explanation on the audit observations presented in paragraph 12.11.

We recommended that the MTO examine the books of more businesses applying for the renewal of their permits in accordance with the provisions of the LGC and LTOM to properly assess the tax liabilities of the said establishments.
The MTO agreed to implement the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BPLO to validate applicants' compliance with other regulations by requiring the applicants for the Mayor’s Permit to submit the applicable documents listed in the LTOM.
The BPLO agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the MTO and BPLO expedite the request for the adoption of an Ordinance for the collection of stickers and plates in addition to the business tax collected to promptly address the current situation’s legality concerns and establish the legal framework required for collecting said fees.

We recommended that the LCE and other concerned offices evaluate the necessity and importance of MENRO and consider creating a plantilla item in the following budget preparation.
Likewise, we reiterated the audit recommendation that the SWMP be synchronized with annual budgets for proper monitoring and evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE and other concerned Offices
assess the manpower allocation within the MAO in relation to the on-going projects being implemented by the said Office;
identify significant issues and concerns potentially affecting the office’s performance and projects’ operations; and
apply effective solutions to address identified problems.
The Management explained that the implementation of audit recommendations on the MAO Projects cannot be committed because it also concerned lack of manpower, on which decisions are bounded with limitations.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the –
Municipal Accountant to warrant the completeness of supporting documents before making any payment/s to contractors/suppliers; and
BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and/or explanation for the audit observations mentioned in paragraph 19.8.
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the MT and Municipal Agriculturist submit complete documents and accounting of the transactions related to the three bank accounts from the time they became depositor up to the present, for evaluation purposes.
The MT and Municipal Agriculturist had submitted documents except for the complete copy of passbooks.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 7160 and MNGAS for LGUs regarding the maintenance of special accounts under the GF and the corresponding preparation of complete financial statements.
The Management did not guarantee compliance with the audit recommendation on the maintenance of special accounts under the GF because hiring of additional manpower is not assured based on their budget.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the HRMO and Accounting Office to submit immediately to the Audit Team the requisite supporting documents as mandated by COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the CNA grant, including the Sangguniang Panlalawigan-reviewed supplemental budget as specified in the DBM BC No. 2023-1, otherwise, the Audit Team will be compelled to suspend in audit the subject transaction/disbursement.
The required documents are submitted in April 2023 and are for evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the GFPS ensure that attribution is made in accordance with the HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Municipality’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD. We also recommended that the GFPS constitute its M & E Team to execute a proper monitoring and evaluation system.
We further recommended that –
detailed guidelines for the dispersal of farm inputs be developed and officially endorsed; and
the General Services/Treasury Office oversee the distribution of farm inputs to the beneficiaries.
The Management agreed to implement all the audit recommendations concerning the gender responsiveness of the Municipality.

We commended the Municipality for its substantial compliance with the withholding and remittance requirements of the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PHIC.
However, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the
HRMO and Accounting Office to exert more effort to lessen, if not to eliminate, discrepancies of their records with the GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PHIC; and
Accounting Office to employ best efforts in recording transactions so that the actual withheld amount for a month will be remitted the following month.
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

2.9	We recommended and Management:
Through the City Treasurer, agreed to submit a certification that the investments are idle funds including details on the specific sources and composition of the amounts invested; otherwise, transfer back the amount and interest to the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account under the General Fund, if funds are not considered idle; and
Through the SP Secretary, agreed to submit SP Resolutions authorizing these investments and approval of the City Mayor, upon determination and certification of the amounts declared as idle funds by the City Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Agriculturist coordinate with the City Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office on the liquidation of the funds utilized and to require the immediate return of the excess balance of completed projects including the unexpended balance to the source agency, the Department of Agriculture.

We recommended and Management, through the City Accountant, agreed to thoroughly review the deductions made pertaining to GSIS premiums/ loan amortization, and ensure that these are remitted intact and in full to the GSIS within the prescribed period. Moreover, we recommended and the City Accountant agreed to reconcile and settle the previous unremitted balances with the GSIS. Also, we recommended and Management agreed to make written representations with the GSIS regarding these contributions.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Assessor to coordinate with and seek technical assistance from the DOF-Bureau of Local Government Finance to fast-track the preparation of the updated SMVs of the City pursuant to Section 212 of the LGC and to instigate the drafting of an Ordinance for consideration of the Sanggunian, incorporating therein the proposed SMV, assessment level and tax rate.
We further recommended and Management agreed to augment the number of qualified personnel assigned at the City Assessor’s Office to meet the minimum personnel requirements stipulated in Section 5 of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations (MRPAAO) and to conduct capacity building interventions to facilitate the conduct of the general revision of real property assessments in adherence to Section 219 of the same Code and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-001, and closely monitor its completion within the reglementary period of three years.

We recommended and Management, through the City Administrator and Fish Port Administrator, agreed to coordinate with the PFDA to evaluate and come up with strategic and development plans, including schemes on reducing operating costs, to improve the operations of the fish port and increase revenue-generating performance to prevent the consistent incurrence of loss of government resources.
Further, we recommended and the City Accountant agreed to submit the required monthly and annual financial and statistical reports with all the supporting papers and to remit the 10 percent Retainer’s Fee of gross income to the PDFA every 15th day of each month as provided in the MOA.

We recommended and Management, through the City Engineer, agreed to submit an acceptable explanation or justification regarding their computation that resulted in the contract cost above COA estimates for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Engineer to provide to the Audit Team the necessary documentation to address the deficiencies noted in the detailed engineering of the project within reasonable time, for further evaluation on the City’s compliance, and final audit decision.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CPDO with the assistance of the City Engineer to faithfully implement the PPAs under the 20 percent Development Fund and require the preparation of the POW/ project designs during the budget preparation phase to give sufficient time for the implementation of the PPAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CPDO to include in the list all on-going PPAs as well as all the projects that are still to be implemented during the reporting year under all funds. Moreover, we recommended and Management agreed that the list include the needed information, such as the project location, project duration including start and completion dates for proper monitoring and evaluation.

hall which are provided with internet connection, and whose
allowances are not covered by the SB Resolution, in order to

those deemed entitled thereto to more conscionable rates, in
consideration of cheaper, prevailing rates/promo packages
offered by telecommunication companies, so as to observe
prudence in the use of limited government funds;
13.14.3. Focus the limited LGU resources in strengthening its internet
connection within the municipal hall instead of giving
individual telephone/communication allowance.

and Accomplishment Report and submit the revised report/s, when
necessary.

accurate balance of the financial statements in keeping with

17.8.2. The Municipal Treasurer, after the Municipal Accountant has
prepared all the necessary adjustments, cause the payment of
any payables to the concerned fund in order to eliminate the
reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

Treasurer:

into the e-TRACS in order to fairly report collections in a given

with the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended the City Accountant to submit the required documents for online access, and prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements monthly in adherence to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to:
Reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries;
Undertake regular and timely review and reconciliation of the accounts in compliance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations; and
Closely monitor the transactions affecting the reciprocal accounts and ensure these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid errors and omissions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the City General Services Officer to:
Maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPCs) for each local road and its components; and
Render a Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) of the LGU concerned at the end of the accounting period – Annex A.
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of the road networks and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Networks (RPCLRN) – Annex C; and
Direct the City Accountant to:
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system following the format provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the City Treasurer and Disbursing Officers:
Payment of salaries, wages, and other personnel benefits be made to the payees’ personal ATM account; and
Granting of cash advances for salaries, wages, other personnel benefits, and other similar items must be equal to the net amount of the payroll for the pay period to minimize, if not eliminate, the excess amount of CA.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to stop the granting of cash advances over the accountable officer’s maximum cash accountability and increase the fidelity bond of accountable officers per schedule provided in Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to stop granting cash advances for operating expenses other than travel, to officials and employees without approved bond.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the City Treasurer to require the DO to update the cashbook daily or upon occurrence of transaction and reconcile the balance with the cash on hand.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Accounting Office to make adjusting entries for the erroneous recording as Advances for Payroll instead of Advances to Special Disbursing Officer.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accounting Office to facilitate the proper recording of the books of the cash advances granted and liquidated.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
Issue a directive to the City Treasurer to implement control measures to avoid instances in the accountable officer holding funds over the existing maximum bond accountability; and
Direct the Accountable Officers to determine the necessity of increasing the fidelity bond of the accountable officers to cover their accountabilities.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the disbursing officer to return the unused cash advance and/or submit all documents to fully liquidate his/her cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002;
Direct the City Accountant to monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
Direct all employees to strictly comply with the provisions of the Circulars of cash advances.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to:
Ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents before the processing of the cash advance and that no cash advance shall be drawn unless the previous cash advance had been properly liquidated and taken up in the books;
Strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of the Circulars pertaining to cash advances.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Disbursing Officer to stop the practice of transferring cash advances to other employees and perform disbursing functions herself.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the City Treasurer and Assessor to improve processes in revenue management and be guided by Section 25 of RA 7160. See that the unearned revenues are collected very soon to finance badly needed and priority projects that will rebound to the benefit of the constituents of the city;
Require the City Treasurer to prepare the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card and to furnish the City Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible to support the recording of the RPT and SET receivables; and
Based on the list, require the City Accountant to draw a JEV to record Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the corresponding deferred income accounts to present fairly the balance of the said receivable accounts in the financial statements.

Management direct the City Treasurer to reevaluate its existing cash requirements, and henceforth ensure compliance with the provisions of the fidelity bond requirement either by increasing the amount of the fidelity bond requirement either by increasing the amount of bond of its accountable officers or by designating additional collectors on the excess accountability under a separate fidelity bonding requirement as deemed appropriate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the recording of the accounts Discount on Real Property Tax - Basic and Discount on Special Education Tax upon collection of Real Property and Special Education Tax which are paid at a discount  pursuant to Section 22 of the Manual on NGAS, in order to fully derecognize at gross amount, the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables, as well as the Deferred Real Property Tax  and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts, and fairly present these items in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exclude as Deposits in Transit, those after cut-off collections on the last working day of the month which were deposited on the succeeding month and the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of reporting in the Report of Collection and Deposits (RCD) those deposits which have deposit slips validated on the succeeding month. We recommended that Management formulate innovative schemes and exercise robust measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305(g) Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and 3 Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 and keep up with the provisions of DOF D.O. No. 006-2015 in order to achieve satisfactory performance in revenue generation.

We recommended that Management revisit the current regular plantilla items of the LGU and propose /create another plantilla position when necessary to avoid hiring of contract of service and job order personnel to perform the duties and responsibilities of regular employee, in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 and limit the number of COS workers to minimize the incurrence of Other General, Janitorial and Security Services expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting journal entry after verifications as to the nature of the transactions recorded under the Accounts Payable pursuant to Sections 111 and 112 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2015-009 in order to fairly present the liability accounts in the financial statements.
.

We recommended that Management continue making representations with the BIR in order to determine the final amount payable to BIR and that the Audit Team be constantly furnished copies of all communications and received by the Management from BIR, for evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan fast track the enactment of the ordinance establishing the DYC, giving it corporate existence, defining the responsibilities of the DTC officials involved and other purposes pursuant to the provisions of Section 305,313 and 123 of RA 7160, and immediately submit it together with all pertinent documents pertaining to DTC operations in order to render regularity and propriety of the local administration over the DTC.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully review the supporting documents for the cash advance for confidential expenses and revisit COA-DBM-DILG-GC-GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No.2015-01 so as to ensure proper release, use and reporting of Confidential Fund.

We recommended that Management immediately create a database of all constituents in the municipality, and conduct periodic updating thereof so as to have readily available data to be used in cases of emergency, disaster or pandemic and submit to the audit team, the complete distribution lists. 

We recommended that henceforth, Management, especially the LGU personnel designated to perform the specific tasks on the inventory and distribution of relief packs, keep a uniform and proforma distribution list which shall be updated upon determination of the exact nature and quantity of items to be distributed, and maintain a complete and accurate record of food packs on hand, for transparency purposes. 

We recommended proper monitoring of relief goods so as to ensure timely distribution thereof to constituents and prevent spoilage thereof and wastage of funds, in order to attain the purpose of DBM LBC Nos. 124 and 125.

We recommended that henceforth, Management formulate sound schemes and exercise measures to achieve realizable target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act No. 7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.

We recommended that Management require the municipal treasurer to observe section 32 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I relative to intact and daily deposit of collection to avoid risk of loss or misappropriation of undeposited collections.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make representation with the LMP-Bohol Chapter officials relative to the proper official receipt due to be issued by the latter.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of effecting payment of goods that lacks the required supporting documents required in pre audit as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-002 to avoid disciplinary action against the concerned agency officials and suspensions of transactions in audit that would lead to disallowances.
